(**IDF**; [Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צבא הגנה לישראל, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Hebrew "Romanization of Hebrew"): *[*Tsva Hagana le-Yisra'el*](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/e/ee/He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg/He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg "File:He-Israeli Defense Forces.ogg")*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Army for the Defense of Israel'), alternatively referred to by the Hebrew-language acronym ***Tzahal*** (צה״ל), is the national [military](/wiki/Military "Military") of the [State of Israel](/wiki/State_of_Israel "State of Israel"). It consists of three service branches: the [Israeli Ground Forces](/wiki/Israeli_Ground_Forces "Israeli Ground Forces"), the [Israeli Air Force](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force"), and the [Israeli Navy](/wiki/Israeli_Navy "Israeli Navy").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It is the sole military wing of the [Israeli security apparatus](/wiki/Israeli_security_forces "Israeli security forces"). The IDF is headed by the [chief of the general staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)"), who is subordinate to the [defense minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)").

The **Israel Defense Forces** (**IDF**; [Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צבא הגנה לישראל, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Hebrew "Romanization of Hebrew"): *[*Tsva Hagana le-Yisra'el*](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/e/ee/He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg/He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:He-Israeli_Defense_Forces.ogg "File:He-Israeli Defense Forces.ogg")*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Army for the Defense of Israel'), alternatively referred to by the Hebrew-language acronym ***Tzahal*** (צה״ל), is the national [military](/wiki/Military "Military") of the [State of Israel](/wiki/State_of_Israel "State of Israel"). It consists of three service branches: the [Israeli Ground Forces](/wiki/Israeli_Ground_Forces "Israeli Ground Forces"), the [Israeli Air Force](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force"), and the [Israeli Navy](/wiki/Israeli_Navy "Israeli Navy").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It is the sole military wing of the [Israeli security apparatus](/wiki/Israeli_security_forces "Israeli security forces"). The IDF is headed by the [chief of the general staff](/wiki/Chief_of_the_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of the General Staff (Israel)"), who is subordinate to the [defense minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defense_(Israel) "Ministry of Defense (Israel)").

On the orders of first prime minister [David Ben-Gurion](/wiki/David_Ben-Gurion "David Ben-Gurion"), the IDF was formed on 26 May 1948 and began to operate as a [conscript military](/wiki/Conscription_in_Israel "Conscription in Israel"), drawing its initial recruits from the already existing paramilitaries of the [Yishuv](/wiki/Yishuv "Yishuv")—namely [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah"), the [Irgun](/wiki/Irgun "Irgun"), and [Lehi](/wiki/Lehi_(militant_group) "Lehi (militant group)"). It was formed shortly after the [Israeli Declaration of Independence](/wiki/Israeli_Declaration_of_Independence "Israeli Declaration of Independence") and has participated in [every armed conflict involving Israel](/wiki/List_of_wars_involving_Israel "List of wars involving Israel"). In the wake of the [1979 Egypt–Israel peace treaty](/wiki/Egypt%E2%80%93Israel_peace_treaty "Egypt–Israel peace treaty") and the [1994 Israel–Jordan peace treaty](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Jordan_peace_treaty "Israel–Jordan peace treaty"), the IDF underwent a significant strategic realignment. Previously spread across various fronts—[Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon") and [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria") in the north, [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan") and [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq "Iraq") in the east, and [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt") in the south—the IDF redirected its focus towards [southern Lebanon](/wiki/Israeli_occupation_of_Southern_Lebanon "Israeli occupation of Southern Lebanon") and the [Palestinian territories](/wiki/Palestinian_territories "Palestinian territories"). In 2000, the IDF withdrew from Southern Lebanon and [in 2005 from Gaza](/wiki/Israeli_disengagement_from_Gaza "Israeli disengagement from Gaza"). Conflict between Israel and Islamist groups based in Gaza, notably [Hamas](/wiki/Hamas "Hamas"), has continued since then. Moreover, notable [Israeli–Syrian border incidents](/wiki/Israeli%E2%80%93Syrian_ceasefire_line_incidents_during_the_Syrian_civil_war "Israeli–Syrian ceasefire line incidents during the Syrian civil war") have occurred frequently since 2011, due to regional instability caused by the [Syrian civil war](/wiki/Syrian_civil_war "Syrian civil war").

Since 1967, the IDF has maintained a [close security relationship with the United States](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93United_States_military_relations "Israel–United States military relations"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) including in [research and development](/wiki/Research_and_development "Research and development") cooperation, with joint efforts on the [F-15I](/wiki/McDonnell_Douglas_F-15E_Strike_Eagle "McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle") and the [Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(Israeli_missile) "Arrow (Israeli missile)") defence system, among others. The IDF is believed to have maintained [an operational nuclear weapons capability since 1967](/wiki/Nuclear_weapons_and_Israel "Nuclear weapons and Israel"), possibly possessing between 80 and 400 [nuclear warheads](/wiki/Nuclear_weapon "Nuclear weapon").[[5]](#cite_note-israelcohen-5) The IDF's actions and policies in the Palestinian territories have faced widespread criticism, with accusations of [repression](/wiki/Oppression "Oppression"), institutionalized [discrimination](/wiki/Anti-Palestinianism "Anti-Palestinianism"), [unlawful killings](/wiki/Unlawful_killing "Unlawful killing") and systematic abuses of [Palestinian rights](/wiki/Human_rights_violations_against_Palestinians_by_Israel "Human rights violations against Palestinians by Israel"), with multiple human rights organizations and scholars accusing the IDF of [genocide](/wiki/Gaza_genocide "Gaza genocide").[[6]](#cite_note-HRW1-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Etymology

The [Israeli cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Israel "Cabinet of Israel") ratified the name "Israel Defense Forces" ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צְבָא הַהֲגָנָה לְיִשְׂרָאֵל), *Tzva haHagana leYisra'el*, literally "the army for the defence of Israel", on 26 May 1948. The other main contender was *Tzva Yisra'el* ([Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): צְבָא יִשְׂרָאֵל). The name was chosen because it conveyed the idea that the army's role was defence and incorporated the name [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah"), the pre-state defensive organization upon which the new army was based.[[9]](#cite_note-ostfeld-name-9) Among the primary opponents of the name were Minister [Haim-Moshe Shapira](/wiki/Haim-Moshe_Shapira "Haim-Moshe Shapira") and the [Hatzohar](/wiki/Hatzohar "Hatzohar") party, both in favor of *Tzva Yisra'el*.[[9]](#cite_note-ostfeld-name-9)

In Israel's [basic law](/wiki/Basic_Laws_of_Israel "Basic Laws of Israel") about the military, the Hebrew name of the IDF is צבא הגנה לישראל, *Tzva Hagana LeYisra'el*, without a definite article on the second word.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The IDF traces its roots to Jewish paramilitary organizations in the [New Yishuv](/wiki/Yishuv "Yishuv"), starting with the [Second Aliyah](/wiki/Second_Aliyah "Second Aliyah") (1904 to 1914).[[11]](#cite_note-11) There had been several such organizations, or in part even older date, such as the "Mahane Yehuda" mounted guards company founded by Michael Halperin in 1891[[12]](#cite_note-post-12) (see [Ness Ziona](/wiki/Ness_Ziona#History "Ness Ziona")), [HaMagen](/wiki/HaMagen "HaMagen") (1915–17),[[13]](#cite_note-Oslo-13) HaNoter[[13]](#cite_note-Oslo-13) (1912–13; see [Zionism: Pre-state self-defense](/wiki/Zionism#Pre-state_self-defense "Zionism")), and the much more consequential (but falsely-claimed "first" such organization), [Bar-Giora](/wiki/Bar-Giora "Bar-Giora"), founded in September 1907. Bar-Giora was transformed into [Hashomer](/wiki/Hashomer "Hashomer") in April 1909, which operated until the [British Mandate of Palestine](/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine "Mandatory Palestine") came into being in 1920. Hashomer was an elitist organization with a narrow scope and was mainly created to protect against criminal gangs seeking to steal property. The [Zion Mule Corps](/wiki/Zion_Mule_Corps "Zion Mule Corps") and the [Jewish Legion](/wiki/Jewish_Legion "Jewish Legion"), both part of the [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army") of [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"), further bolstered the Yishuv with military experience and manpower, forming the basis for later paramilitary forces.[[14]](#cite_note-auto4-14)

After the [1920 Palestine riots](/wiki/1920_Palestine_riots "1920 Palestine riots") against Jews in April 1920, the Yishuv leadership realized the need for a nationwide underground defence organization, and the [Haganah](/wiki/Haganah "Haganah") was founded in June 1920.[[14]](#cite_note-auto4-14) The Haganah became a full-scale defence force after the [1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine](/wiki/1936%E2%80%931939_Arab_revolt_in_Palestine "1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine") with an organized structure, consisting of three main units—the [Field Corps](/wiki/Hish_(Haganah_corps) "Hish (Haganah corps)"), [Guard Corps](/wiki/Guard_Corps_(Haganah) "Guard Corps (Haganah)"), and the [Palmach](/wiki/Palmach "Palmach"). During World War II, the Yishuv participated in the British war effort, culminating in the formation of the [Jewish Brigade](/wiki/Jewish_Brigade "Jewish Brigade"). These would eventually form the backbone of the Israel Defense Forces, and provide it with its initial manpower and doctrine.

Following Israel's [Declaration of Independence](/wiki/Declaration_of_Independence_(Israel) "Declaration of Independence (Israel)"), Prime Minister and Defense Minister [David Ben-Gurion](/wiki/David_Ben-Gurion "David Ben-Gurion") issued an order for the formation of the Israel Defense Forces on 26 May 1948. Although Ben-Gurion had no legal authority to issue such an order, the order was made legal by [the cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Israel "Cabinet of Israel") on 31 May. The same order called for the disbandment of all other Jewish armed forces.[[15]](#cite_note-ostfeld-legal-15) The two other Jewish underground organizations, [Irgun](/wiki/Irgun "Irgun") and [Lehi](/wiki/Lehi_(group) "Lehi (group)"), agreed to join the IDF if they would be able to form independent units and agreed not to make independent arms purchases. This was the background for the [Altalena Affair](/wiki/Altalena_Affair "Altalena Affair"), a confrontation surrounding weapons purchased by the Irgun resulting in a standoff between Irgun members and the newly created IDF. The affair came to an end when *Altalena*, the ship carrying the arms, was shelled by the IDF. Following the affair, all independent Irgun and Lehi units were either disbanded or merged into the IDF. The Palmach, a leading component of the Haganah, also joined the IDF [with provisions](/wiki/The_Generals%27_Revolt "The Generals' Revolt"), and Ben Gurion responded by disbanding its staff in 1949, after which many senior Palmach officers retired, notably its first commander, [Yitzhak Sadeh](/wiki/Yitzhak_Sadeh "Yitzhak Sadeh").

The new army organized itself when the [1947–48 Civil War in Mandatory Palestine](/wiki/1947%E2%80%9348_Civil_War_in_Mandatory_Palestine "1947–48 Civil War in Mandatory Palestine") escalated into the [1948 Arab–Israeli War](/wiki/1948_Arab%E2%80%93Israeli_War "1948 Arab–Israeli War"), which saw neighbouring Arab states attack. Twelve [infantry](/wiki/Infantry_Corps_(Israel) "Infantry Corps (Israel)") and [armoured](/wiki/Armor_Corps_(Israel) "Armor Corps (Israel)") brigades formed: [Golani](/wiki/Golani_Brigade "Golani Brigade"), [Carmeli](/wiki/Carmeli_Brigade "Carmeli Brigade"), [Alexandroni](/wiki/Alexandroni_Brigade "Alexandroni Brigade"), [Kiryati](/wiki/Kiryati_Brigade "Kiryati Brigade"), [Givati](/wiki/Givati_Brigade "Givati Brigade"), [Etzioni](/wiki/Etzioni_Brigade "Etzioni Brigade"), the [7th](/wiki/7th_Armoured_Brigade_(Israel) "7th Armoured Brigade (Israel)"), and [8th](/wiki/8th_Armored_Brigade_(Israel) "8th Armored Brigade (Israel)") armoured brigades, [Oded](/wiki/Oded_Brigade "Oded Brigade"), [Harel](/wiki/Harel_Brigade "Harel Brigade"), [Yiftach](/wiki/Yiftach_Brigade "Yiftach Brigade"), and [the Negev](/wiki/Negev_Brigade "Negev Brigade").[[16]](#cite_note-idf-enc-11-15-16) After the war, some of the brigades were converted to reserve units, and others were disbanded. Directorates and corps were created from corps and services in the Haganah, and this basic structure in the IDF [still exists today](#Organization).

Immediately after the 1948 war, the [Israel-Palestinian conflict](/wiki/Israel-Palestinian_conflict "Israel-Palestinian conflict") shifted to a [low-intensity conflict](/wiki/Reprisal_operations "Reprisal operations") between the IDF and [Palestinian fedayeen](/wiki/Palestinian_fedayeen "Palestinian fedayeen"). In the 1956 [Suez Crisis](/wiki/Suez_Crisis "Suez Crisis"), the IDF's first serious test of strength after 1949, the new army captured the [Sinai Peninsula](/wiki/Sinai_Peninsula "Sinai Peninsula") from Egypt, which was later returned. In the 1967 [Six-Day War](/wiki/Six-Day_War "Six-Day War"), Israel conquered the Sinai Peninsula, [Gaza Strip](/wiki/Gaza_Strip "Gaza Strip"), [West Bank](/wiki/West_Bank "West Bank") (including [East Jerusalem](/wiki/East_Jerusalem "East Jerusalem")) and [Golan Heights](/wiki/Golan_Heights "Golan Heights") from the surrounding Arab states, changing the balance of power in the region as well as the role of the IDF. In the following years leading up to the [Yom Kippur War](/wiki/Yom_Kippur_War "Yom Kippur War"), the IDF fought in the [War of Attrition](/wiki/War_of_Attrition "War of Attrition") against Egypt in the Sinai and a border war against the [Palestine Liberation Organization](/wiki/Palestine_Liberation_Organization "Palestine Liberation Organization") (PLO) in [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), culminating in the [Battle of Karameh](/wiki/Battle_of_Karameh "Battle of Karameh").

The surprise of the Yom Kippur War and its aftermath completely changed the IDF's procedures and approach to warfare. Organizational changes were made and more time was dedicated to training for [conventional warfare](/wiki/Conventional_warfare "Conventional warfare"). However, in the following years the army's role slowly shifted again to low-intensity conflict, [urban warfare](/wiki/Urban_warfare "Urban warfare") and [counter-terrorism](/wiki/Counter-terrorism "Counter-terrorism"). An example of the latter was the successful 1976 [Operation Entebbe](/wiki/Operation_Entebbe "Operation Entebbe") commando raid to free hijacked airline passengers being held captive in [Uganda](/wiki/Uganda "Uganda"). During this era, the IDF also mounted a [successful bombing mission](/wiki/Operation_Opera "Operation Opera") in [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq "Iraq") to destroy its nuclear reactor. It was involved in the [Lebanese Civil War](/wiki/Lebanese_Civil_War "Lebanese Civil War"), initiating [Operation Litani](/wiki/Operation_Litani "Operation Litani") and later the [1982 Lebanon War](/wiki/1982_Lebanon_War "1982 Lebanon War"), where the IDF ousted Palestinian guerrilla organizations from [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon").

For twenty-five years the IDF maintained a [security zone](/wiki/Israeli_occupation_of_southern_Lebanon "Israeli occupation of southern Lebanon") inside South Lebanon with their allies the [South Lebanon Army](/wiki/South_Lebanon_Army "South Lebanon Army"). Palestinian militancy has been the main focus of the IDF ever since, especially during the [First](/wiki/First_Intifada "First Intifada") and [Second Intifadas](/wiki/Second_Intifada "Second Intifada"), [Operation Defensive Shield](/wiki/Operation_Defensive_Shield "Operation Defensive Shield"), the [Gaza War (2008–2009)](/wiki/Gaza_War_(2008%E2%80%9309) "Gaza War (2008–09)"), the [2012 Gaza War](/wiki/Operation_Pillar_of_Defense "Operation Pillar of Defense"), the [2014 Gaza War](/wiki/Operation_Protective_Edge "Operation Protective Edge"), and the [2021 Israel-Palestine crisis](/wiki/Operation_Guardian_of_the_Walls "Operation Guardian of the Walls"), causing the IDF to change many of its values and publish the [IDF Code of Ethics](/wiki/IDF_Code_of_Ethics "IDF Code of Ethics"). The Lebanese [Shia](/wiki/Shia_Islam "Shia Islam") organization [Hezbollah](/wiki/Hezbollah "Hezbollah") has also been a growing threat,[[17]](#cite_note-17) against which the IDF fought an [asymmetric conflict](/wiki/South_Lebanon_conflict_(1982%E2%80%932000) "South Lebanon conflict (1982–2000)") between 1982 and 2000, as well as a [full-scale war](/wiki/2006_Lebanon_War "2006 Lebanon War") in 2006.

The Israel Defense Forces have been accused of committing various [war crimes](/wiki/Israeli_war_crimes "Israeli war crimes") since the [founding of Israel](/wiki/Israeli_Declaration_of_Independence "Israeli Declaration of Independence") in 1948. Israel ratified the [Geneva Conventions](/wiki/Geneva_Conventions "Geneva Conventions") on July 6, 1951,[[18]](#cite_note-HRW-18) and on January 2, 2015, the [State of Palestine](/wiki/State_of_Palestine "State of Palestine") acceded to the [Rome Statute](/wiki/Rome_Statute "Rome Statute"), granting the [International Criminal Court](/wiki/International_Criminal_Court "International Criminal Court") (ICC) jurisdiction over war crimes committed in the [Occupied Palestinian Territories](/wiki/Palestinian_territories "Palestinian territories") (OPT).[[19]](#cite_note-19) A 2017 report by [Human Rights Watch](/wiki/Human_Rights_Watch "Human Rights Watch") accused the IDF of [unlawful killings](/wiki/Unlawful_killing "Unlawful killing"), [using excessive force in policing situations](/wiki/Police_brutality "Police brutality"), [forced displacement](/wiki/Forced_displacement "Forced displacement"), excessive use of [detention](/wiki/Detention_(imprisonment) "Detention (imprisonment)") and [excessive restrictions on movement](/wiki/Palestinian_freedom_of_movement "Palestinian freedom of movement"), as well as criticized the IDF's support and protection for [Israeli settlements](/wiki/Israeli_settlements "Israeli settlements") in the [occupied Palestinian territory](/wiki/Occupied_Palestinian_territory "Occupied Palestinian territory").[[6]](#cite_note-HRW1-6)
Human rights experts argue that actions taken by the IDF during armed conflicts in the OPT fall under the rubric of war crimes.[[20]](#cite_note-20) Various [UN special rapporteurs](/wiki/UN_special_rapporteur "UN special rapporteur"), alongside human rights and aid organizations including [Human Rights Watch](/wiki/Human_Rights_Watch "Human Rights Watch"), [Médecins Sans Frontières](/wiki/M%C3%A9decins_Sans_Fronti%C3%A8res "Médecins Sans Frontières"), [Amnesty International](/wiki/Amnesty_International "Amnesty International"), have accused Israel of war crimes, and since 2024, [genocide](/wiki/Genocide "Genocide").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-MSF_war_crimes1-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Organization

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Honor_Guard_at_IDF_Headquarters_for_Outgoing_Chief_of_Staff_Lt._Gen._Gabi_Ashkenazi.jpg/250px-Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Honor_Guard_at_IDF_Headquarters_for_Outgoing_Chief_of_Staff_Lt._Gen._Gabi_Ashkenazi.jpg)](/wiki/File:Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Honor_Guard_at_IDF_Headquarters_for_Outgoing_Chief_of_Staff_Lt._Gen._Gabi_Ashkenazi.jpg)

IDF [Kirya Compound](/wiki/HaKirya "HaKirya"), Tel Aviv

All branches of the IDF answer to a single [General Staff](/wiki/General_Staff_(Israel) "General Staff (Israel)"). The [Chief of the General Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of General Staff (Israel)") is the only serving officer having the rank of [Lieutenant General](/wiki/Lieutenant_General "Lieutenant General") (*Rav Aluf*). He reports directly to the [Defense Minister](/wiki/Defense_Minister_of_Israel "Defense Minister of Israel") and indirectly to the [Prime Minister of Israel](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Israel "Prime Minister of Israel") and the cabinet. Chiefs of Staff are formally appointed by the cabinet, based on the Defense Minister's recommendation, for three years. The government can vote to extend their service to four, and on rare occasions even five years. The current chief of staff is [Eyal Zamir](/wiki/Eyal_Zamir "Eyal Zamir").[[28]](#cite_note-28)

#### Personnel Directorate

The Israeli Personnel Directorate (Hebrew: אגף כוח אדם, Agaf Koakh Adam, abbreviated to AKA), formerly called the Manpower Directorate and the Human Resources Directorate, is the Israel Defense Forces body that holds responsibility for planning and coordination of Human Resources placement and movement within the IDF, planning and management of all military Human Resources, and responsibility for the welfare of all service members. The current Head of the Personnel Directorate is Major General Dado Bar Kalifa.

The Personnel Directorate has eight main corps and General Officer-level units directly subordinate to it, as well as numerous units and sub-units. The six main subordinates are:

* [Military Police Corps](/wiki/Military_Police_Corps_(Israel) "Military Police Corps (Israel)")
* [Education and Youth Corps](/wiki/Education_and_Youth_Corps_(Israel) "Education and Youth Corps (Israel)")
* [Human Resources Corps and Casualties Division](/wiki/Adjutant_Corps "Adjutant Corps")
* [Gender Advisor to the Chief of Defense Staff](/wiki/Gender_Affair_Advisor_to_the_Chief_of_Staff "Gender Affair Advisor to the Chief of Staff")
* [Staff Division and General Corps](/wiki/General_Corps_(Israel) "General Corps (Israel)")
* Human Resources Planning and Management Division
  + [Meitav](/wiki/Meitav_(military_unit) "Meitav (military unit)")
* Welfare Division
  + MOFET
* Behavioral Science Center

### Uniforms

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/Idf-uniform-en.png/250px-Idf-uniform-en.png)](/wiki/File:Idf-uniform-en.png)

IDF uniform colors

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Israeli_Honor_Guards_stand_at_attention_01.jpg/250px-Israeli_Honor_Guards_stand_at_attention_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Israeli_Honor_Guards_stand_at_attention_01.jpg)

IDF female Military Police wearing skirts with their white caps and belts

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/20/Spike_LR_3.jpg/250px-Spike_LR_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Spike_LR_3.jpg)

Female IDF corporal with the [Spike missile](/wiki/Spike_(missile) "Spike (missile)") launcher, wearing the golden-olive Madei Alef uniform

The Israel Defense Forces has several types of uniforms:

* [Service dress](/wiki/Service_dress "Service dress") (מדי אלף *Madei Alef* – Uniform "A") – the everyday uniform, worn by everybody.
* [Field dress](/wiki/Battledress "Battledress") (מדי ב *Madei Bet* – Uniform "B") – worn into combat, training, work on base.

The first two resemble each other but the Madei Alef is made of higher quality materials in a golden olive while the madei bet is in [olive drab](/wiki/Olive_drab "Olive drab").[[30]](#cite_note-Katz_1986-30)[[31]](#cite_note-historama.com-31) The dress uniforms may also exhibit a surface shine[[31]](#cite_note-historama.com-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)

* Officers / Ceremonial dress (מדי שרד *madei srad*) – worn by officers, or during special events/ceremonies.
* [Dress uniform](/wiki/Dress_uniform "Dress uniform") and [mess dress](/wiki/Mess_dress "Mess dress") – worn only abroad. Several dress uniforms are depending on the season and the branch.

The service uniform for all ground forces personnel is [olive green](/wiki/Olive_green "Olive green"); navy and air force uniforms are [beige](/wiki/Beige "Beige")/[tan](/wiki/Tan_(color) "Tan (color)") (also once worn by the ground forces). The uniforms consist of a two-pocket shirt, [combat trousers](/wiki/Combat_trousers "Combat trousers"), [sweater](/wiki/Sweater "Sweater"), jacket or blouse, and shoes or boots. The navy also has an all-white dress uniform. The green fatigues are the same for winter and summer and heavy winter gear is issued as needed. Women's dress parallels the men's but may substitute a skirt for trousers and a blouse for a shirt.

Headgear included a service cap for dress and semi-dress and a [field cap](/wiki/Field_cap "Field cap") or ["Kova raful" bush hat](/wiki/Boonie_hat "Boonie hat") worn with fatigues. Many IDF personnel once wore the [tembel](/wiki/Tembel_hat "Tembel hat") as a field hat. IDF personnel generally wear [berets](/wiki/Beret "Beret") instead of the service cap and there are many beret colours issued to IDF personnel. [Paratroopers](/wiki/Paratroopers_Brigade "Paratroopers Brigade") are issued a maroon beret, [Golani](/wiki/Golani_Brigade "Golani Brigade") brown, [Givati](/wiki/Givati_Brigade "Givati Brigade") purple, [Nahal](/wiki/Nahal_Brigade "Nahal Brigade") lime green, [Kfir](/wiki/Kfir_Brigade "Kfir Brigade") camouflage, Combat Engineers grey, navy blue for IDF Naval and dark grey for IDF Air Force personnel.

In combat uniforms, the [Orlite helmet](/wiki/Orlite_helmet "Orlite helmet") has replaced the British [Brodie helmet Mark II/Mark III](/wiki/Brodie_helmet "Brodie helmet"), [RAC Mk II modified helmet with chin web jump harness](/wiki/RAC_helmet "RAC helmet") (used by [paratroopers](/wiki/Paratroopers_Brigade "Paratroopers Brigade") and similar to the [HSAT Mk II/Mk III paratrooper helmets](/wiki/Helmet_Steel_Airborne_Troop "Helmet Steel Airborne Troop")),[[33]](#cite_note-33) US [M1 helmet](/wiki/M1_helmet "M1 helmet"),[[34]](#cite_note-34) and French [Modèle 1951 helmet](/wiki/Mod%C3%A8le_1951_helmet "Modèle 1951 helmet") – previously worn by Israeli infantry and airborne troops from the late 1940s to the mid-1970s and early 1980s.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Some corps or units have small variations in their uniforms – for instance, [military police](/wiki/Military_Police_Corps_(Israel) "Military Police Corps (Israel)") wear a white belt and [police hat](/wiki/Peaked_cap "Peaked cap"), Naval personnel have dress whites for parades, paratroopers are issued a four pocket tunic (yarkit/yerkit) worn untucked with a pistol belt cinched tight around the waist over the shirt.[[36]](#cite_note-auto2-36) The IDF Air Corps has a dress uniform consisting of a pale blue shirt with dark blue trousers.

Most IDF soldiers are issued black leather [combat boots](/wiki/Combat_boots "Combat boots"), certain units issue [reddish-brown](/wiki/Russet_(color) "Russet (color)") leather boots for historical reasons — the paratroopers,[[36]](#cite_note-auto2-36) combat medics, Nahal and Kfir Brigades, as well as some [Special Forces units](/wiki/Israeli_special_forces_units "Israeli special forces units") ([Sayeret Matkal](/wiki/Sayeret_Matkal "Sayeret Matkal"), [Oketz](/wiki/Unit_Oketz "Unit Oketz"), [Duvdevan](/wiki/Duvdevan_Unit "Duvdevan Unit"), [Maglan](/wiki/Maglan "Maglan"), and the Counter-Terror School). Women were also formerly issued [sandals](/wiki/Sandal_(footwear) "Sandal (footwear)"), but this practice has ceased.

### Insignia

Main article: [Israel Defense Forces insignia](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_insignia "Israel Defense Forces insignia")

IDF soldiers have three types of insignia (other than rank insignia) which identify their corps, specific unit, and position. A [pin attached to the beret](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_insignia#Beret_pins "Israel Defense Forces insignia") identifies a soldier's corps. Individual units are identified by a shoulder tag attached to the left [shoulder strap](/wiki/Shoulder_strap_(insignia) "Shoulder strap (insignia)"). The position/job of a soldier can then be identified by an [aiguillette](/wiki/Aiguillette "Aiguillette") attached to the left shoulder strap and shirt pocket, and a pin indicating the soldier's work type.

## Service

### Military service routes

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_163rd_IAF_Flight_Course_Graduates_%281%29.jpg/250px-Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_163rd_IAF_Flight_Course_Graduates_%281%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_163rd_IAF_Flight_Course_Graduates_(1).jpg)

163rd IAF flight course graduates (2011)

The military service is held in three different tracks:

* **[Regular service](/wiki/National_service "National service")** (שירות חובה): mandatory military service which is held according to the Israeli security service law.
* **[Permanent service](/wiki/Military_recruitment "Military recruitment")** (שירות קבע): military service which is held as part of a contractual agreement between the IDF and the permanent position-holder.
* **[Reserve service](/wiki/Reserve_duty_(Israel) "Reserve duty (Israel)")** (שירות מילואים): a military service in which citizens are called for active duty of at most a month every year (in accordance with the Reserve Service Law), for training and ongoing military activities and especially to increase the military forces in case of a war.

Sometimes the IDF would also hold pre-military courses (קורס קדם צבאי or קד"צ) for soon-to-be regular service soldiers.
