### United Kingdom

Further information: [Israel–United Kingdom relations](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93United_Kingdom_relations "Israel–United Kingdom relations")

The United Kingdom has supplied equipment and spare parts for [Sa'ar 4.5-class missile boats](/wiki/Sa%27ar_4.5-class_missile_boat "Sa'ar 4.5-class missile boat") and [F-4 Phantom](/wiki/F-4_Phantom "F-4 Phantom") fighter-bombers, components for small-calibre artillery ammunition and air-to-surface missiles, and engines for [Elbit Hermes 450](/wiki/Elbit_Hermes_450 "Elbit Hermes 450") [Unmanned aerial vehicles](/wiki/Unmanned_aerial_vehicle "Unmanned aerial vehicle"). British arms sales to Israel mainly consist of light weaponry, and ammunition and components for helicopters, tanks, armoured personnel carriers, and combat aircraft.[[198]](#cite_note-198)[[199]](#cite_note-199)

### Russia

Further information: [Israel–Russia relations § Military collaboration](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Russia_relations#Military_collaboration "Israel–Russia relations")

On 19 October 1999, the Defense Minister of China, General [Chi Haotian](/wiki/Chi_Haotian "Chi Haotian"), after meeting with Syrian Defense Minister [Mustafa Tlass](/wiki/Mustafa_Tlass "Mustafa Tlass") in [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus"), Syria, to discuss expanding military ties between Syria and China, then flew directly to Israel and met with [Ehud Barak](/wiki/Ehud_Barak "Ehud Barak"), the then Prime Minister and Defense Minister of Israel where they discussed military relations. Among the military arrangements was a $1 billion Israeli–Russian sale of military aircraft to China, which were to be jointly produced by Russia and Israel.[[200]](#cite_note-200)

Russia has bought drones from Israel.[[201]](#cite_note-201)[[202]](#cite_note-202)[[203]](#cite_note-203)[[204]](#cite_note-204)[[205]](#cite_note-205)

### China

Further information: [China–Israel relations](/wiki/China%E2%80%93Israel_relations "China–Israel relations")

Israel is the second-largest foreign supplier of arms to the People's Republic of China, only after the [Russian Federation](/wiki/Russian_Federation "Russian Federation"). China has purchased a wide array of military hardware from Israel, including [Unmanned aerial vehicles](/wiki/Unmanned_aerial_vehicle "Unmanned aerial vehicle") and [communications satellites](/wiki/Communications_satellite "Communications satellite"). China has become an extensive market for Israel's military industries and arms manufacturers, and trade with Israel has allowed it to obtain "dual-use" technology which the United States and [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") were reluctant to provide.[[206]](#cite_note-206) In 2010 [Yair Golan](/wiki/Yair_Golan "Yair Golan"), head of [IDF Home Front Command](/wiki/Home_Front_Command "Home Front Command") visited China to strengthen military ties.[[207]](#cite_note-207) In 2012, [IDF Chief of Staff](/wiki/Chief_of_General_Staff_(Israel) "Chief of General Staff (Israel)") [Benny Gantz](/wiki/Benny_Gantz "Benny Gantz") visited China for high-level talks with the Chinese defence establishment.[[208]](#cite_note-208)

### Cyprus

Further information: [Cyprus–Israel relations](/wiki/Cyprus%E2%80%93Israel_relations "Cyprus–Israel relations")

As closely neighbouring countries, Israel and Cyprus have enjoyed greatly improving diplomatic relations since 2010. During the [Mount Carmel Forest Fire](/wiki/Mount_Carmel_forest_fire_(2010) "Mount Carmel forest fire (2010)"), Cyprus dispatched two aviation assets to assist fire-fighting operations in Israel – the first time Cypriot Government aircraft were permitted to operate from Israeli airfields in a non-civil capacity.[[209]](#cite_note-209) Israel and Cyprus have closely cooperated in maritime activities relating to Gaza, since 2010, and have reportedly begun an extensive sharing program of regional intelligence to support mutual security concerns. In May 2012, it was widely reported that the Israeli Air Force had been granted unrestricted access to the Nicosia Flight Information Region of Cyprus and that Israeli aviation assets may have operated over the island itself.[[210]](#cite_note-210)

### Greece

Further information: [Greece–Israel relations](/wiki/Greece%E2%80%93Israel_relations "Greece–Israel relations")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/94/Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Apache_Helicopters_Overlooking_Greece.jpg/250px-Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Apache_Helicopters_Overlooking_Greece.jpg)](/wiki/File:Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Apache_Helicopters_Overlooking_Greece.jpg)

Two IAF [Apache AH-64D Longbows](/wiki/Boeing_AH-64_Apache "Boeing AH-64 Apache") and one Greek AH-64A fly above the Greek countryside during a joint exercise, June 2011.

Since 2010, the Israeli and Greek air forces trained jointly in Greece, indicating a boost in ties due in large part to Israel's rift with Turkey.[[211]](#cite_note-211) Recent purchases include 100 million euro deal between Greece and Israel for the purchase of [SPICE](/wiki/Spice_(guidance_kit) "Spice (guidance kit)") 1000 and SPICE 2000 pound bomb kits.[[212]](#cite_note-212) In November 2011, the [Israeli Air Force](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force") hosted Greece's [Hellenic Air Force](/wiki/Hellenic_Air_Force "Hellenic Air Force") in a joint exercise at the [Uvda base](/wiki/Ovda_Airport "Ovda Airport").[[213]](#cite_note-213)[[214]](#cite_note-214) Similar training was held in 2012 by the IAF in cooperation with the Hellenic Air Force in the [Peloponnese](/wiki/Peloponnese "Peloponnese") and parts of southern Greece in a response to the need of the IAF training of pilots in unfamiliar areas.[[215]](#cite_note-215)[[216]](#cite_note-216) On March 14, 2013, the navies of Israel, Greece and the US held a two-week joint military exercise for the third year in a row. The annual operation is nicknamed Noble Dina and was established in 2011. Similar to Noble Dina in 2012, the exercise in 2013 included defending offshore natural gas platforms and simulated air-to-air combat and anti-submarine warfare.[[217]](#cite_note-217)[[218]](#cite_note-218)[[219]](#cite_note-219) In March 2017, Israel participated in the large-scale "Iniochus 2017" military exercise, which is organized annually by Greece, along with USA, Italy and the United Arab Emirates.[[220]](#cite_note-220)[[221]](#cite_note-221)

### Turkey

Further information: [Israel–Turkey relations](/wiki/Israel%E2%80%93Turkey_relations "Israel–Turkey relations")

Israel has provided extensive military assistance to Turkey. Israel sold Turkey [IAI Heron](/wiki/IAI_Heron "IAI Heron") [Unmanned aerial vehicles](/wiki/Unmanned_aerial_vehicle "Unmanned aerial vehicle"), and modernized Turkey's [F-4 Phantom](/wiki/F-4_Phantom "F-4 Phantom") and [Northrop F-5](/wiki/Northrop_F-5 "Northrop F-5") aircraft at the cost of $900 million. Turkey's [main battle tank](/wiki/Main_battle_tank "Main battle tank") is the Israeli-made [Sabra](/wiki/Sabra_(tank) "Sabra (tank)") tank, of which Turkey has 170. Israel later upgraded them for $500 million. Israel has also supplied Turkey with Israeli-made missiles, and the two nations have engaged in naval cooperation. Turkey allowed Israeli pilots to practice long-range flying over mountainous terrain in Turkey's Konya firing range, while Israel trains Turkish pilots at Israel's computerized firing range at [Nevatim Airbase](/wiki/Nevatim_Airbase "Nevatim Airbase").[[222]](#cite_note-222)[[223]](#cite_note-223) Until 2009, the Turkish military was one of Israel's largest defence customers. Israel defence companies have sold unmanned aerial vehicles and long-range targeting pods.[[224]](#cite_note-224)

However, relations have been strained in recent times. Since 2010 the Turkish military has declined to participate in the annual joint naval exercise with Israel and the United States.[[225]](#cite_note-225)[[226]](#cite_note-226) The exercise, known as "Reliant Mermaid" was started in 1998 and included the Israeli, Turkish and American navies.[[227]](#cite_note-227) The objective of the exercise is to practice search-and-rescue operations and to familiarize each navy with international partners who also operate in the [Mediterranean Sea](/wiki/Mediterranean_Sea "Mediterranean Sea").[[228]](#cite_note-228)

### Azerbaijan

Further information: [Azerbaijan–Israel relations](/wiki/Azerbaijan%E2%80%93Israel_relations "Azerbaijan–Israel relations")

Azerbaijan and Israel have engaged in intense cooperation since 1992.[[229]](#cite_note-229) Israeli military have been a major provider of battlefield aviation, artillery, antitank, and anti-infantry weaponry to Azerbaijan.[[230]](#cite_note-230)[[231]](#cite_note-231) In 2009, Israeli President [Shimon Peres](/wiki/Shimon_Peres "Shimon Peres") made a visit to Azerbaijan where military relations were expanded further, with the Israeli company [Aeronautics Defense Systems Ltd](/wiki/Aeronautics_Defense_Systems_Ltd "Aeronautics Defense Systems Ltd") announcing it was going to build a factory in [Baku](/wiki/Baku "Baku").[[232]](#cite_note-232)

In 2012, Israel and Azerbaijan signed an agreement according to which state-run [Israel Aerospace Industries](/wiki/Israel_Aerospace_Industries "Israel Aerospace Industries") would sell $1.6 billion in drones and anti-aircraft and missile defense systems to Azerbaijan.[[233]](#cite_note-233) In March 2012, the magazine *[Foreign Policy](/wiki/Foreign_Policy "Foreign Policy")* reported that the [Israeli Air Force](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force") may be preparing to use the [Sitalchay Military Airbase](/wiki/Sitalchay_Military_Airbase "Sitalchay Military Airbase"), located 500 km (310 mi) from the Iranian border, for air strikes against the [nuclear program of Iran](/wiki/Nuclear_program_of_Iran "Nuclear program of Iran"),[[234]](#cite_note-FP_2012-234) later backed up by other media.[[235]](#cite_note-235)

### Other countries

Israel has also sold to or received supplies of military equipment from the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"), [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina"), [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"), [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"), [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro"), [Bosnia and Herzegovina](/wiki/Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Bosnia and Herzegovina"),[[236]](#cite_note-amnesty-236) [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(country) "Georgia (country)"),[[237]](#cite_note-237) [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam") and [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"),[[238]](#cite_note-238) among others.

### Parades

Main article: [Israel Defense Forces parade](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_parade "Israel Defense Forces parade")

Israel Defense Forces parades took place on Independence Day, during the first 25 years of the State of Israel's existence. They were cancelled after 1973 due to financial and security concerns. The Israel Defense Forces still have weapon exhibitions country-wide on Independence Day, but they are stationary.

### Commemoration

See also: [Yom Hazikaron](/wiki/Yom_Hazikaron "Yom Hazikaron")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7b/Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Remembering_the_Fallen.jpg/250px-Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Remembering_the_Fallen.jpg)](/wiki/File:Flickr_-_Israel_Defense_Forces_-_Remembering_the_Fallen.jpg)

An IDF ceremony for Yom Hazikaron

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/PikiWiki_Israel_12768_IDF_parade_in_Jerusalem_in_1968.jpg/250px-PikiWiki_Israel_12768_IDF_parade_in_Jerusalem_in_1968.jpg)](/wiki/File:PikiWiki_Israel_12768_IDF_parade_in_Jerusalem_in_1968.jpg)

Israeli female soldiers on parade, Jerusalem, 1968

[Yom Hazikaron](/wiki/Yom_Hazikaron "Yom Hazikaron"), Israel's day of remembrance for fallen soldiers, is observed on the 4th day of the month of [Iyar](/wiki/Iyar "Iyar") of the [Hebrew calendar](/wiki/Hebrew_calendar "Hebrew calendar"), the day before the celebration of [Independence Day](/wiki/Yom_Ha%27atzmaut "Yom Ha'atzmaut").

The main museum for Israel's armoured corps is the [Yad La-Shiryon](/wiki/Yad_La-Shiryon "Yad La-Shiryon") in [Latrun](/wiki/Latrun "Latrun"), which houses one of the largest tank museums in the world. Other significant military museums are the [Israel Defense Forces History Museum](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces_History_Museum "Israel Defense Forces History Museum") (Batei Ha-Osef) in [Tel Aviv](/wiki/Tel_Aviv "Tel Aviv"), the [Palmach Museum](/wiki/Palmach_Museum "Palmach Museum"), and the Beit HaTotchan of artillery in [Zikhron Ya'akov](/wiki/Zikhron_Ya%27akov "Zikhron Ya'akov"). The [Israeli Air Force Museum](/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force_Museum "Israeli Air Force Museum") is located at [Hatzerim Airbase](/wiki/Hatzerim_Airbase "Hatzerim Airbase") in the [Negev Desert](/wiki/Negev_Desert "Negev Desert"), and the Israeli Clandestine Immigration and Naval Museum, is in [Haifa](/wiki/Haifa "Haifa").

Israel's [National Military Cemetery](/w/index.php?title=Mount_Herzl_Military_Cemetery&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mount Herzl Military Cemetery (page does not exist)") [[he](https://he.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D7%91%D7%99%D7%AA_%D7%94%D7%A7%D7%91%D7%A8%D7%95%D7%AA_%D7%94%D7%A6%D7%91%D7%90%D7%99_%D7%91%D7%94%D7%A8_%D7%94%D7%A8%D7%A6%D7%9C "he:בית הקברות הצבאי בהר הרצל")] is at [Mount Herzl](/wiki/Mount_Herzl "Mount Herzl"). Other Israeli military cemeteries include [Kiryat Shaul Military Cemetery](/wiki/Kiryat_Shaul_Military_Cemetery "Kiryat Shaul Military Cemetery") in Tel Aviv, and Sgula military cemetery at [Petah Tikva](/wiki/Petah_Tikva "Petah Tikva").
